steps.title: Pinpoint the problem Text 1 raises and what Text 2 actually does
steps.content:
Text 1 states that countries define urban settlements using different population thresholds, making cross-country comparisons unreliable. Text 2 describes how six international organizations created a standardized "degree of urbanization" measure with global criteria for cities, towns, and rural areas — a direct solution to the inconsistency problem.
  • Choice A — By suggesting that researchers focus on topics besides urbanization: Text 2 does not suggest abandoning urbanization research; it improves urbanization research tools. ✗
  • Choice B — By noting that a possible solution to the problem is available: The "degree of urbanization" measure Text 2 describes is exactly a solution to the inconsistency problem Text 1 raises. ✓
  • Choice C — By recommending that a specific institution should further investigate the problem: Text 2 does not call for more investigation; it presents a measure already developed. ✗
  • Choice D — By supplying additional ways in which urbanization research is difficult: Text 2 reduces difficulty rather than adding to it. ✗

steps.title: Match Text 2's purpose to the choices
steps.content: Text 2 presents a concrete tool — the "degree of urbanization" standard — that resolves the cross-country comparison problem introduced in Text 1. That is a solution, not a new problem, a recommendation for more research, or a pivot to a different topic. That matches Choice B — By noting that a possible solution to the problem is available.
